summary: Comprehensive testing of ReversePicks auth system after critical bug fix. The bug was `setPassword` (React state setter) being called instead of `apiSetPassword` (API function) in handleSetPassword on line 289. Fix confirmed and all auth flows working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: Health endpoint returns 200 OK
Backend: Owner auto-login (josselj001@gmail.com) returns verified=true with session token
Backend: Non-member (nobody@test.com) returns verified=false with 'No active membership' message
Backend: Lifetime sub (rijulgauchan1@gmail.com) returns requires_password or requires_password_setup
Backend: Set password for lifetime sub returns verified=true with session token
Backend: Short password (<6 chars) returns 400 error
Backend: Login with correct password returns verified=true
Backend: Login with wrong password returns 401 error
Backend: Login without password set returns 401 error
Backend: Session verification with valid token returns valid=true
Backend: Session verification with invalid token returns valid=false
Backend: Logout invalidates session correctly
Frontend: Login page loads correctly with email input and verify button
Frontend: Owner auto-login bypasses password and shows main app with Predict/Tracking/Chat tabs
Frontend: Logout returns to login page
Frontend: Lifetime sub shows 'Membership Verified' badge and password form
Frontend: Wrong password shows 'Invalid password' error
Frontend: Correct password logs in and shows main app
Frontend: Non-member shows 'No active membership found' error
Frontend: Session persists after page refresh
critical_code_review_comments: CONFIRMED FIX: Line 13 imports `setPassword as apiSetPassword` and Line 289 correctly calls `apiSetPassword(email, password)` instead of the React state setter
seed_data_creation: Password set for rijulgauchan1@gmail.com during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth system fully tested. Owner auto-login, lifetime sub password setup/login, non-member rejection, session persistence, and logout all working. The critical bug fix (apiSetPassword vs setPassword) has been verified. rijulgauchan1@gmail.com now has password 'testpass1' set in the database.

bug_fix_verification.bug_description: React state setter `setPassword` was being called instead of API function `apiSetPassword` in handleSetPassword
bug_fix_verification.verification_method: Code review confirmed import alias on line 13 and correct function call on line 289. Frontend testing confirmed password setup flow works correctly.
